name: Polabian

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Polabian (pola1255).

notes: Skeleton. West Slavic (Lechitic) language of the Elbe, extinct since the 18th century and known only from German-authored word lists and glossaries. The grapheme map follows the scholarly reconstruction transcription (Latin base with diacritics for the reduced/rounded vowels å ü ö ə, the nasals ą/ǫ, and the palatal sibilant/affricate series š ž č ś ź ć ń). Because the primary records were noted down with inconsistent German spelling conventions, the reconstructed values are uncertain; stress and vowel reduction patterns are only partly recoverable and are left unencoded, so an elevated PER floor is expected.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Polabian (pola1255)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
sources.notes: Classification reference for a skeleton-tier entry.
